subject: Economy
stem: Which statement is correct about the composition of national income in India?
options: The share of the manufacturing sector has declined.
The share of the services sector has increased sharply.
The share of agriculture has remained static.
The share of the services sector has declined.
explanation: Over the decades India's economy has shifted toward services, whose share of GDP has risen sharply (now well over half). Agriculture's share has steadily fallen (not static), and services have grown, not declined.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Trans-Pacific Partnership (TPP):
subs: 1. Signed by 12 Pacific Rim nations in 2015.
2. Member countries account for about 40% of global GDP.
3. India is a founder member of TPP.
explanation: [Defective] The TPP was signed on 4 February 2016 (negotiations concluded 2015), so 'signed in 2015' (statement 1) is wrong as printed. Statement 2 is correct (TPP members were about 40% of world GDP), and India was never a member (statement 3 false). Only statement 2 is strictly correct, but '2 only' is not among the options - so the question is defective.

subject: Economy
stem: The base year of India's new GDP series was shifted from 2004-05 to
explanation: India revised the GDP base year to 2011-12 (in the 2015 revision), also moving to GVA-based measurement at market prices.

subject: Economy
stem: Per updated World Bank data for 2017, India is the sixth biggest economy by GDP. Which one is not ahead of India?
explanation: India overtook France to become the world's sixth-largest economy; Japan, the UK and Germany were still ahead at that time. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Indexation is a method associated with which of the following?
explanation: Indexation links payments (wages, pensions, dearness allowance) to a price index so they automatically rise with inflation, protecting real incomes.

subject: Economy
stem: [Tax receipts as % of GDP:
subs: 1. Corporation Tax
2. Income Tax
3. GST
4. Union Excise Duties.] Which is the correct descending order of these tax receipts as a percentage of GDP?
explanation: As per the 2019-20 estimates, the descending order was Corporation Tax > GST > Income Tax > Union Excise Duties (1, 3, 2, 4). (A data-recall item; confirm against the year's budget figures.)

subject: Economy
stem: As per the World Bank Ease of Doing Business ranking, India improved from 142 (2014) to 63 (2019). In which parameter did India's rank deteriorate?
explanation: While India improved sharply on most indicators, its rank on "Registering Property" stagnated/slipped over this period. (A data-recall item worth confirming against the original key.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the National Food Security Act is not correct?
options: The Act was rolled out in 2014.
It legally entitles 67% of the population to highly subsidised foodgrains.
The Act is not being implemented in all the States/UTs.
explanation: By the time of the question the NFSA had been implemented across all States and UTs, so statement d is the incorrect one. The Act (2013) covers about two-thirds (67%) of the population.

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the Indian economy during 2019-20 is not correct?
options: There was deceleration in the growth rate.
Tax revenue grew sluggishly relative to Budget Estimates.
The fiscal deficit as a percentage of GDP was as per the Budget Estimates.
Non-tax revenue registered considerably higher growth.
explanation: In 2019-20 the fiscal deficit overshot the Budget Estimate (slipping to ~3.8% against the budgeted ~3.3% of GDP), so statement c is not correct.

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement is not correct?
options: Real GDP is calculated by valuing outputs of different years at common prices.
Potential GDP is the real GDP the economy would produce with fully employed resources.
Nominal GDP is calculated by valuing outputs of different years at constant prices.
Real GDP per capita is real GDP divided by population.
explanation: Nominal GDP uses current (year's own) prices, not constant prices — so statement c is false. Real GDP is the one that uses constant/base-year prices.

subject: Economy
stem: A situation where equilibrium real GDP falls short of potential GDP is known as
explanation: When actual output is below potential (full-employment) output, the shortfall is a recessionary (deflationary) gap, indicating under-utilised resources.

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about social-sector spending in India during 2014-19 (States + Union together) is true?
options: Health spending was 4% of social-services expenditure.
There was stagnation in education spending as a percent of GDP.
Health-sector spending was 10% of total expenditure.
explanation: Education expenditure as a share of GDP remained roughly flat (around 3%) over this period — a stagnation. The other figures are incorrect.

subject: Economy
stem: Which indicator is included in the National Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)?
explanation: The MPI uses indicators across health, education and standard of living — including years of schooling (and school attendance). Income, literacy rate and life expectancy are not among its specific indicators.

subject: Economy
stem: 1. The GDP deflator captures the average price of an unchanging basket of commodities.
subs: 2. The GDP deflator can measure real GDP but not the inflation rate.
explanation: The GDP deflator covers all goods/services in current output (a changing basket) — the "fixed basket" is the CPI — so statement 1 is wrong. And the deflator can measure inflation (it is itself a price index), so statement 2 is wrong too.

subject: Economy
stem: Per capita GDP depends upon:
subs: 1. Proportion of population in the working age.
2. Work participation rate.
3. Per-worker productivity.
explanation: Per capita GDP = (working-age share) × (participation rate) × (output per worker), so it depends on all three factors.

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement is not correct for National Income Accounting in India?
options: Imports are subtracted in calculating GDP.
Net factor payments earned from abroad are included in GDP.
Purchase/sale of second-hand goods are not included in GDP.
Inventories are included in Gross Domestic Capital Formation.
explanation: Net factor income from abroad is added to GDP to get GNP — it is not part of GDP itself — so statement b is false. The other three are correct accounting conventions.

subject: Economy
stem: About innovation and R&D in India:
subs: 1. India's Global Innovation Index rank improved significantly between 2015 and 2024.
2. Effective capital expenditure as % of GDP increased from 2020-21 to 2023-24.
explanation: India's GII ranking rose sharply (from the 80s toward the 39–40 range) over 2015–2024 (1), and effective capital expenditure as a share of GDP increased in this period (2). (Statements 3 and 4 in the source are not part of the answer options.)

subject: Economy
stem: 1. Effective capital expenditure as % of GDP increased from 2020-21 to 2023-24.
subs: 2. Expenditure on revenue account as % of GDP increased from 2020-21 to 2023-24.
explanation: Effective capital expenditure rose as a share of GDP (1, correct), reflecting the capex push, while revenue expenditure as a share of GDP fell (2, incorrect) as the government consolidated.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is a good statistic to evaluate where an economy stands in the financial cycle?
explanation: The credit-to-GDP ratio (and its "gap") is the standard indicator of the financial cycle — rising credit signals expansion/build-up of risk, falling credit signals contraction.

subject: Economy
stem: According to the Classical Theory of Employment, deviations from full employment are
explanation: Classical economists held that the economy automatically tends to full employment, so any unemployment is only a temporary disequilibrium corrected by flexible wages/prices.

subject: Economy
stem: Capital deepening refers to
options: going for more fixed capital per worker
explanation: Capital deepening means raising the amount of capital per worker (more machines/tools per worker), boosting labour productivity.

subject: Economy
stem: The Twelfth Plan focused on inclusive growth. Which were considered challenges for inclusiveness?
subs: 1. Poverty
2. Group inequality
3. Regional imbalance
4. Unemployment.
explanation: The Twelfth Plan identified poverty, group inequality, regional imbalance and unemployment as the key challenges to inclusive growth — all four.

subject: Economy
stem: The phenomenon of demographic dividend relates to
options: a sharp decline in total population
a decline in infant mortality rate
explanation: The demographic dividend is the growth potential from a rising share of working-age population relative to dependents.

subject: Economy
stem: Arrange in sequence:
subs: 1. Mahalanobis Model
2. Plan Holiday
3. Rolling Plan.
explanation: Chronologically: Mahalanobis Model (Second Plan, 1956) → Plan Holiday (1966-69) → Rolling Plan (1978).

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the DDU-GKY is not correct?
options: It is a placement-linked skill-training programme exclusively for rural girls.
It aims to convert India's demographic surplus into a demographic dividend.
It aims to benefit more than 55 million poor rural folk.
It is a generational poverty-alleviation programme.
explanation: DDU-GKY is for rural youth (both young men and women), not exclusively girls — so statement a is false.

subject: Economy
stem: The natural rate of unemployment hypothesis was advocated by
explanation: Milton Friedman (with Edmund Phelps) proposed the natural rate of unemployment hypothesis, arguing there is no permanent inflation-unemployment trade-off.

subject: Economy
stem: When some goods or productive factors are completely fixed in amount regardless of price, the supply curve is
explanation: If quantity supplied cannot change with price (perfectly inelastic supply), the supply curve is a vertical line.

subject: Economy
stem: A market situation where many firms sell similar but not identical products is termed
explanation: Monopolistic competition has many sellers offering differentiated (similar but not identical) products, each with limited pricing power.

subject: Economy
stem: According to John Maynard Keynes, employment depends upon
explanation: Keynes held that the level of employment is determined by effective demand (the point where aggregate demand equals aggregate supply).

subject: Economy
stem: What is meant by Demographic Dividend?
options: A rise in economic growth due to a higher share of working-age people
A rise in literacy due to educational institutions
A rise in living standards due to alternative livelihoods
A rise in the employment ratio due to government policies
explanation: The demographic dividend is faster economic growth arising from a larger working-age share of the population.

subject: Economy
stem: The value of the slope of a normal demand curve is
explanation: By the law of demand, quantity demanded falls as price rises, so the demand curve slopes downward (negative slope).

subject: Economy
stem: Match: A. Perfect competition B. Monopoly C. Monopolistic competition D. Oligopoly |
subs: 1. One producer selling one commodity
2. Few producers selling similar/almost similar products
3. Many producers selling differentiated products
4. Many producers selling similar products.
explanation: Perfect competition → many sellers of homogeneous (similar) products (4); monopoly → single seller (1); monopolistic competition → differentiated products (3); oligopoly → few sellers (2).

subject: Economy
stem: Normally, there will not be a shift in the demand curve when
options: consumers want to buy more at any given price
explanation: A change in the commodity's own price causes movement along the demand curve, not a shift. Shifts come from changes in income, population or tastes.

subject: Economy
stem: A market with a large number of firms, a homogeneous product, infinite elasticity of demand for an individual firm and no price control is
explanation: These are the textbook features of perfect competition — many firms, identical product, price-taking firms facing perfectly elastic demand.

subject: Economy
stem: When did the East India Company lose its monopoly of the China trade?
explanation: The Charter Act of 1833 ended the Company's remaining trade monopoly, including the China (tea) trade. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which may lead to a movement along the demand curve of a commodity?
options: Change in price of other commodities
explanation: Only a change in the good's own price causes movement along its demand curve; the other factors shift the whole curve.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is a typical example of monopolistic competition?
explanation: The soap market has many sellers offering branded, differentiated products — the hallmark of monopolistic competition.

subject: Economy
stem: Who first mooted the idea of deficit financing?
explanation: Keynes advocated deficit financing — government spending in excess of revenue — to stimulate demand during recessions.

subject: Economy
stem: Which factors signify monopolistic competition?
subs: 1. Differentiated products
2. Large number of buyers and sellers
3. Barriers to entry
4. Homogeneous products.
explanation: Monopolistic competition features differentiated products and many buyers/sellers (1, 2) with free entry/exit (so no barriers, 3 wrong) and differentiated, not homogeneous, products (4 wrong).

subject: Economy
stem: Which is/are UN Sustainable Development Goals?
subs: 1. Decent work and economic growth
2. Peace, justice and strong institutions
3. Responsible consumption and production.
explanation: All three are SDGs — Goals 8, 16 and 12 respectively.

subject: Economy
stem: Which cannot be a feature of a totalitarian regime?
options: State control of the political and personal realms
explanation: A totalitarian regime suppresses civil society, so its autonomy cannot be a feature. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: With a normal downward-sloping demand curve and a fully elastic (horizontal) supply curve, an exogenous decrease in demand leads to
options: increase in equilibrium price and quantity
decrease in equilibrium price and quantity
decrease in equilibrium quantity and no change in price
increase in equilibrium price and no change in quantity
explanation: With perfectly elastic (horizontal) supply, price is fixed; a fall in demand simply reduces the equilibrium quantity while the price stays unchanged.

subject: Economy
stem: What was the focus of the Twelfth Five-Year Plan?
explanation: The Twelfth Plan's theme was "Faster, More Inclusive and Sustainable Growth."

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about a firm's equilibrium in perfect competition is not correct?
options: Market price must be ≥ average variable cost in the short run.
Market price must equal marginal cost.
Market price must equal average cost in the long run.
Marginal cost decreases at the equilibrium output.
explanation: At a profit-maximising equilibrium, the firm produces where MC is rising (cuts MR/price from below), not falling — so statement d is false.

subject: Economy
stem: Which pair of firm and feature is not correctly matched?
options: Monopolistic firm – Firm is a price setter
Monopoly firm – Produces an efficient level of output
Perfectly competitive firm – Produces socially optimum output
explanation: A monopoly produces less than the socially optimal output at a higher price (it is allocatively inefficient) — so this pairing is wrong. The others are correctly matched.

subject: Economy
stem: Which index is now used by the Reserve Bank of India to measure the rate of inflation?
explanation: Since the move to inflation targeting (2016), the RBI uses CPI (Combined) headline inflation as its key measure (target 4% ± 2%).

subject: Economy
stem: Match: A. Lorenz curve B. Phillips curve C. Engel curve D. Laffer curve |
subs: 1. Inflation and employment
2. Tax rates and tax revenue
3. Inequality in income/wealth distribution
4. Income and proportion of expenditure on food.
explanation: Lorenz → income/wealth inequality (3); Phillips → inflation vs unemployment (1); Engel → income vs share spent on food (4); Laffer → tax rate vs tax revenue (2).

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not one of the Eight Core Industries?
explanation: The Eight Core Industries are coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilizers, steel, cement and electricity — rubber products is not among them.

subject: Economy
stem: Which situation can lead to inflation?
options: Rapid growth of aggregate demand outweighing supply
explanation: Demand-pull inflation occurs when aggregate demand grows faster than supply, pulling prices up. The other options are disinflationary.

subject: Economy
stem: Which measure can the Government use to combat inflation?
options: Increasing non-plan expenditure on defence, police, etc.
Increasing the rate of interest on savings and fixed deposits
explanation: Higher interest rates encourage saving and reduce spending, curbing demand-pull inflation. The other options would expand demand/money supply and worsen inflation.

subject: Economy
stem: Effects of devaluation/depreciation of currency:
subs: 1. Increase in imports and decrease in exports.
2. Increase in exports and decrease in imports.
3. Increase in domestic inflation.
4. Decrease in domestic inflation.
explanation: A weaker currency makes exports cheaper and imports dearer (so exports rise, imports fall — 2) and raises the price of imported goods, fuelling domestic inflation (3).

subject: Economy
stem: Wholesale Price inflation rose in India during 2021-22 due to:
subs: 1. Sharp rise in international crude oil prices.
2. Decrease in economic activity post-Covid.
3. Disruption of global supply chains.
4. High freight cost.
explanation: Rising crude prices (1), supply-chain disruption (3) and high freight costs (4) drove WPI inflation; economic activity was recovering, not falling, so statement 2 is wrong.

subject: Economy
stem: Regarding the weightage of articles in the Wholesale Price Index (WPI):
subs: 1. Fuel and power have higher weightage in WPI primary articles.
2. The weightage of manufactured products in WPI is higher than that of fuel and power.
explanation: Fuel & Power is a separate WPI group, not part of "primary articles" (so 1 is wrong). Manufactured products carry the largest weight (~64%), far more than fuel & power (~13%) — so 2 is correct.

subject: Economy
stem: While calculating the Consumer Price Index, housing as an item is classified as
explanation: Housing is treated as a service in the CPI and is part of "core" inflation (excluding food and fuel).

subject: Economy
stem: Core inflation is
options: CPI headline inflation minus all non-food items
WPI inflation minus all food and non-food items
CPI headline inflation minus food and energy items
WPI inflation minus food and energy items
explanation: Core inflation strips out the volatile food and fuel/energy components from headline CPI to reveal the underlying price trend.

subject: Economy
stem: In India, the term "hot money" refers to
explanation: "Hot money" is short-term, volatile Foreign Portfolio Investment that can flow in and out quickly chasing returns, unlike long-term FDI.

subject: Economy
stem: Devaluation of currency will be more beneficial if
options: prices of domestic goods remain constant
explanation: Devaluation works mainly by making a country's exports cheaper for foreign buyers and imports costlier for domestic buyers. Option b directly captures this benefit (exports become cheaper to importers).

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not correct about the Directorate of Enforcement?
options: It is a specialized financial investigation agency under the Department of Revenue, Ministry of Finance.
It enforces the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999.
It enforces the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002.
It enforces the Prohibition of Benami Property Transaction Act, 1988.
explanation: The ED enforces FEMA and the PMLA; the Benami Property Transactions Act is administered by the Income-Tax authorities, not the ED — so statement d is incorrect.

subject: Economy
stem: The sustained decrease in the general price level is called
explanation: Deflation is a sustained fall in the general price level (negative inflation).

subject: Economy
stem: Which government actions would contract the money supply?
subs: 1. Purchase of Treasury Bills by the central bank from the public.
2. Sale of Treasury Bills by the central bank to the public.
3. Sale of foreign exchange by the central bank.
4. Purchase of foreign exchange by the central bank.
explanation: Selling T-bills (2) and selling foreign exchange (3) both absorb rupees from the system, contracting money supply. Buying T-bills or forex (1, 4) injects money.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not included in the Capital Account of the Balance of Payments?
explanation: "Invisibles" (services, transfers, income) are part of the Current Account, not the Capital Account. FDI, commercial borrowings and external assistance are capital-account items.

subject: Economy
stem: How can currency depreciation stimulate an increase in net exports?
subs: 1. By reducing export costs.
2. By reducing import prices.
explanation: Depreciation makes a country's exports cheaper for foreign buyers (1), boosting them. It raises (not reduces) import prices, so statement 2 is wrong.

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement is not correct?
options: Creation of the NIIF was announced in the Union Budget 2015-16.
NIIF is a fund for enhancing infrastructure.
NIIF and NIF (National Investment Fund) are the same organization.
NIIF can have more than one alternative investment fund.
explanation: The NIIF and the NIF are different bodies — the NIIF is an infrastructure-financing fund, while the NIF holds disinvestment proceeds — so statement c is false.

subject: Economy
stem: Match: A. Fiscal Deficit B. Revenue Deficit C. Effective Revenue Deficit D. Primary Deficit |
subs: 1. Total Expenditure − Revenue Receipts − Non-debt Capital Receipts
2. Revenue Expenditure − Revenue Receipts
3. Revenue Deficit − Grants for Creation of Capital Assets
4. Fiscal Deficit − Interest Payments.
explanation: Fiscal Deficit = Total Exp − (Revenue Receipts + Non-debt Capital Receipts) (1); Revenue Deficit = Revenue Exp − Revenue Receipts (2); Effective Revenue Deficit = Revenue Deficit − Grants for Capital Assets (3); Primary Deficit = Fiscal Deficit − Interest Payments (4).

subject: Economy
stem: UBI, as an alternative for subsidies in poverty alleviation, stands for
explanation: UBI = Universal Basic Income — an unconditional periodic cash payment to all citizens, discussed as a possible replacement for subsidies.

subject: Economy
stem: Consider the following statements:
subs: Statement I: The overall fiscal deficit of the States stayed above the FRBM threshold of 3% for the third successive year in 2017-18.
Statement II: Special Category States ran a higher fiscal deficit in 2017-18 than in 2016-17.
options: Both the statements are individually true and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I
Both the statements are individually true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
explanation: The overall fiscal deficit of the States in 2017-18 fell below the 3% FRBM threshold, so Statement I is false. Special Category States did run a higher fiscal deficit in 2017-18 than in 2016-17, so Statement II is true. Hence Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

subject: Economy
stem: The Kyoto Protocol commits State parties to reduction in
explanation: The Kyoto Protocol binds developed countries to cut greenhouse-gas emissions.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is an example of a price floor?
options: Minimum Support Price (MSP) for Jowar in India
explanation: A price floor is a government-set minimum price above the market level; the MSP guarantees farmers a minimum price, so it is a classic price floor. (MRP is a price ceiling.)

subject: Economy
stem: Saubhagya, a Government of India scheme, relates to
explanation: Saubhagya (Pradhan Mantri Sahaj Bijli Har Ghar Yojana) aimed at universal household electrification by providing last-mile electricity connections.

subject: Economy
stem: As per the 2019-20 Budget Estimates, the maximum expenditure on major subsidies was likely on
explanation: The food subsidy is consistently the largest of the major subsidies.

subject: Economy
stem: Which expenditure is subtracted from the Fiscal Deficit to arrive at the Primary Deficit?
explanation: Primary Deficit = Fiscal Deficit − Interest Payments — it shows the deficit excluding the burden of past borrowing.

subject: Economy
stem: Which item comprises the major portion of the Union Government's revenue expenditure?
explanation: Interest payments on accumulated government debt form the single largest component of revenue expenditure.

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement is correct about the GST Bill passed by the Rajya Sabha in August 2016?
options: It will replace all central taxes only by a single tax.
It will subsume central as well as State taxes, duties, etc.
GST will be levied on alcoholic liquor at a uniform 25%.
Petroleum and petroleum products shall not be subjected to GST.
explanation: The defining feature of GST is that it subsumes both central taxes (excise, service tax) and State taxes (VAT, etc.) into one tax. Alcohol is excluded; petroleum is within GST's ambit but kept zero-rated initially.

subject: Economy
stem: The TRIPS Agreement pertains to
explanation: TRIPS (Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights) is the WTO agreement setting minimum standards for IP protection.

subject: Economy
stem: The Most Favoured Nation (MFN) clause under the WTO is based on the principle of
options: differential treatment between locals and foreigners
explanation: Under MFN, a country must extend any trade advantage given to one member to all WTO members equally — the principle of non-discrimination among nations.

subject: Economy
stem: The Goods and Services Tax is not a
explanation: GST is a value-added, consumption-based, destination-based tax (with input tax credit), so it is not a gross value tax.

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the Convener of the Task Force (Nov 2017) to review the Income-tax Act and draft a new direct tax law?
explanation: CBDT member Arbind Modi convened the 2017 task force on a new direct-tax code.

subject: Economy
stem: Which equals Personal Disposable Income?
options: Personal Income − direct taxes paid by households and miscellaneous fees, fines, etc.
Private Income − Saving of Private Corporate Sectors − Corporation Tax
Total household expenditure − Income Tax − Gifts received
explanation: Personal Disposable Income = Personal Income − direct taxes − miscellaneous fees/fines paid by households — the income actually available to spend or save.

subject: Economy
stem: [Sources of tax receipts, 2019-20 BE]
subs: 1. Corporation Tax
explanation: Corporation tax (tax on company profits) is one of the major direct-tax sources listed for the descending-order question; in 2019-20 it was among the largest contributors. (The source split one question into separate numbered items.)

subject: Economy
stem: [Sources of tax receipts, 2019-20 BE]
subs: 2. Taxes on Income other than Corporation Tax
explanation: This is personal income tax, another major direct-tax source in the ranking question above.

subject: Economy
stem: [Sources of tax receipts, 2019-20 BE]
subs: 3. Goods and Services Tax
explanation: GST is the major indirect-tax source listed; together with corporation and income tax it forms the set ranked in the descending-order question.

subject: Economy
stem: Following the 101st Amendment (2016), Parliament enacted several GST Acts in 2017. Which does not fall in this category?
options: The Central Goods and Services Tax Act
The Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act
The State Goods and Services Tax Act
explanation: Parliament enacted the CGST, IGST, UTGST and Compensation Acts; the State GST Acts are passed by individual State Legislatures, not Parliament.

subject: Economy
stem: What is "itat e-dwar"?
options: An e-filing portal of the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal
explanation: "itat e-dwar" is the e-filing portal of the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT). (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is considered an agency function of commercial banks in India?
explanation: Agency functions are services banks perform on behalf of customers/government — such as collecting income-tax payments. Accepting deposits and giving overdrafts are primary (banking) functions.

subject: Economy
stem: Which tax is not subsumed under the Goods and Services Tax in India?
options: Taxes on Petroleum and Petroleum Products
explanation: Petroleum and petroleum products remain outside the regular GST levy, so the official-style answer is d. But note Basic Customs Duty is also NOT subsumed under GST, whereas the additional customs duties (CVD and SAD) WERE subsumed - so 'customs duties' is ambiguous, making a single clean answer unsafe.

subject: Economy
stem: If India enters FTAs, its export growth would depend upon:
subs: 1. Extent of tariff reduction vis-à-vis MFN tariffs.
2. Relaxation in rules of origin.
3. Relaxation in sanitary/phytosanitary measures.
4. Level of infrastructure in India.
5. Income in partner nations.
explanation: Export gains from FTAs depend on all these — tariff cuts, rules-of-origin and SPS relaxations, India's own infrastructure, and the income/demand in partner countries.

subject: Economy
stem: Which tax is not included in the Central Pool shared with States as per the Finance Commission?
explanation: Surcharges and cesses are not part of the divisible pool shared with States — the Centre retains them — a frequent point of friction in Centre-State finance.

subject: Economy
stem: Which Constitution Amendment Act introduced GST in the course of inter-state trade or commerce?
explanation: The 101st Amendment Act, 2016 introduced GST, adding Article 246A and Article 269A (which governs IGST on inter-state trade).

subject: Economy
stem: About Bitcoin:
subs: 1. It is a decentralized virtual currency.
2. It is generated through complex computer software.
3. The RBI recognized it as legal tender in January 2016.
explanation: Bitcoin is a decentralized digital currency (1) "mined" by computers (2). The RBI has never made it legal tender, so statement 3 is false.

subject: Economy
stem: Monetary policy in India uses which tools?
subs: 1. Bank rate
2. Open market operations
3. Public debt
4. Public revenue.
explanation: The bank rate and open-market operations are monetary-policy tools of the RBI. Public debt and public revenue are fiscal-policy matters of the government.

subject: Economy
stem: The RBI's high-level task force on the Public Credit Registry was headed by
explanation: The 2017 task force on the Public Credit Registry was chaired by Y. M. Deosthalee.

subject: Economy
stem: Per the RBI's study on State finances, capital spending is maximum on
explanation: Among the listed heads, States' capital spending was reported highest on rural development. (A data-recall item worth confirming against the original key.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who chaired the RBI's Committee on Deepening Digital Payments?
explanation: The RBI's Committee on Deepening Digital Payments (2019) was chaired by Nandan Nilekani.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not correct about the Repo rate?
options: It is the interest rate charged by the central bank on overnight loans.
It is the rate paid by commercial banks on overnight borrowing.
It is the rate agreed in the loan contract between a commercial bank and the central bank.
It is the cost of collateral security.
explanation: The repo rate is the interest rate at which the RBI lends to banks against securities — it is not the "cost of collateral," so statement d is false.

subject: Economy
stem: The Cash Reserve Ratio refers to
options: the share of NDTL banks hold as liquid assets
the share of NDTL banks hold as balances with the RBI
the share of NDTL banks hold as part of their cash reserves
the ratio of cash holding to reserves of banks
explanation: The CRR is the percentage of a bank's Net Demand and Time Liabilities (NDTL) that it must keep as cash balances with the RBI (earning no interest). (Liquid assets held by the bank itself relate to the SLR.)

subject: Economy
stem: The RBI recently imposed withdrawal limits (initially for six months) on account holders of which bank?
explanation: In 2019 the RBI capped withdrawals from the Punjab and Maharashtra Cooperative (PMC) Bank after a major fraud came to light. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which policy mix raises the interest rate unambiguously and leads to currency appreciation?
options: Contractionary fiscal and expansionary monetary policy
Contractionary monetary and expansionary fiscal policy
explanation: Contractionary monetary policy raises rates directly, and expansionary fiscal policy (more borrowing) also pushes rates up — together they raise interest rates unambiguously, attracting capital inflows and appreciating the currency.

subject: Economy
stem: Which are included in Narrow Money (M1)?
subs: 1. Currency with the public
2. Demand deposits
3. Other deposits with the RBI
4. Bankers' deposits with the RBI.
explanation: M1 = currency with the public + demand deposits + "other deposits" with the RBI. Bankers' deposits with the RBI (4) are excluded from M1.

subject: Economy
stem: Regarding monetary-policy instruments:
subs: 1. The Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) rate was introduced in April 2022.
2. The SDF rate replaced the fixed reverse repo rate as the floor of the LAF corridor.
explanation: The SDF was introduced in April 2022 and now forms the floor of the Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) corridor, replacing the fixed reverse repo rate — both statements are correct.

subject: Economy
stem: The four great needs of the people as identified by the Guomindang were
options: education, food, housing and health care
employment, housing, education and health care
explanation: Sun Yat-sen's Guomindang identified the people's four great needs as clothing, food, housing and transportation. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is correct about the term "bank run"?
options: The net balance of money a bank has at day's end
The ratio of total deposits to total liabilities
A panic situation when depositors start withdrawing cash
The period in which a bank creates the highest credit
explanation: A "bank run" occurs when many depositors, fearing the bank's failure, rush to withdraw their money simultaneously.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is/are credit rating agency/agencies in India?
explanation: CRISIL, CARE and ICRA are all credit-rating agencies operating in India.

subject: Economy
stem: About the India Post Payments Bank (IPPB):
subs: 1. It was incorporated as a Public Limited Company.
2. It started by establishing two pilot branches at Hyderabad and Varanasi.
explanation: IPPB was incorporated as a public limited company (1). Its pilot branches were launched at Raipur and Ranchi, not Hyderabad and Varanasi — so statement 2 is wrong.

subject: Economy
stem: Benefits of the Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY):
subs: 1. Free treatment at all public and empanelled private hospitals.
2. Cashless and paperless access.
3. Health cover up to ₹5,00,000 per family per year.
4. Pre-existing diseases are not covered.
explanation: PMJAY offers free, cashless, paperless treatment up to ₹5 lakh per family per year (1, 2, 3). Crucially, pre-existing conditions ARE covered, so statement 4 is false.

subject: Economy
stem: In September 2019, which travel giant declared itself bankrupt?
explanation: The 178-year-old British travel firm Thomas Cook collapsed in September 2019. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is the latest addition to the AYUSH group of health-care systems?
explanation: Sowa-Rigpa (a traditional Tibetan/Himalayan medicine system) is the most recent addition to AYUSH. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: NIDHI is an umbrella scheme for the promotion of
explanation: NIDHI (National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations) supports innovators and startups. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) was established in
explanation: The IBBI was set up in 2016 under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016.

subject: Economy
stem: Objectives of the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojana (PMSSY):
subs: 1. Correcting regional imbalances in tertiary health care.
2. Universal access to public health and universal immunization.
3. Augmenting facilities for quality medical education.
explanation: PMSSY aims to correct regional imbalances in tertiary care (1) (via new AIIMS-like institutions) and augment medical education (3). Universal immunization (2) is not its objective. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: In line with NEP 2020, "Poshan bhi Padhai bhi" (PBPB) was launched under which programme?
options: Integrated Teacher Training Programme Vidya Pravesh
explanation: PBPB is the national framework for Early Childhood Care and Education (ECCE) at anganwadis, aligned with NEP 2020. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: What is the maximum age for joining the National Pension System (NPS) under the NPS–Private Sector?
explanation: At the time of the question the maximum entry age for NPS (private sector) had been raised to 65 years (it was later extended to 70). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is/are social-security schemes?
subs: 1. Atal Pension Yojana
2. Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
3. Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana.
explanation: All three — APY (pension), PMJJBY (life insurance) and PMSBY (accident insurance) — are social-security schemes launched in 2015. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about an Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF) is not correct?
options: It experiences price changes throughout the day.
It typically has lower daily liquidity and higher fees than mutual fund shares.
An ETF does not have its NAV calculated once at the end of every day.
explanation: ETFs generally have higher liquidity and lower fees than mutual funds (and trade throughout the day like shares) — so statement c is false.

subject: Economy
stem: Which event is not correctly matched with its year?
options: Inauguration of the SWIFT system of electronic interbank fund transfers – 1985
Conclusion of the Uruguay Round of GATT – 1994
Inauguration of the World Trade Organization – 1995
Establishment of the first wholly electronic stock exchange, Nasdaq – 1971
explanation: SWIFT went live in 1977, not 1985 — so this pairing is wrong. The Uruguay Round (1994), WTO (1995) and Nasdaq (1971) dates are correct.

subject: Economy
stem: Which sector is not affected by the changes made in the FDI Policy in June 2016?
options: Manufacturing of small arms and ammunition covered under the Arms Act, 1959
explanation: The June 2016 FDI liberalisation touched defence, private security, civil aviation, pharma, etc., but left the multi-brand retail policy unchanged. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which would be considered Foreign Direct Investment?
options: A foreign company buying shares on Indian stock exchanges
A foreign pension fund investing in Indian stock markets
A foreign merchant banker buying shares from Indian markets
A foreign entity setting up an educational institution in India
explanation: FDI involves a lasting interest and management control — e.g. setting up a physical institution/business. Buying shares in the market is portfolio investment (FPI/FII), not FDI.

subject: Economy
stem: The members of NAFTA are
explanation: The North American Free Trade Agreement was among the USA, Canada and Mexico (since superseded by the USMCA).

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the Niryat Bandhu Scheme is correct?
options: It is a scheme for mentoring first-generation entrepreneurs.
It is for the vulnerable sections of society.
It is for monitoring the rural poor.
explanation: The Niryat Bandhu Scheme (DGFT) mentors new and first-generation entrepreneurs in international trade. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: About the Make in India initiative:
subs: 1. It was launched in 2018.
2. Its objective is to foster innovation.
explanation: Make in India was launched in 2014, not 2018 (so 1 is wrong); fostering manufacturing and innovation is among its objectives (2 correct).

subject: Economy
stem: About ASEAN:
subs: 1. The Chairmanship rotates annually based on the alphabetical order of the English names of Member States.
2. The Chairmanship is decided by voting.
3. "One Vision, One Identity, One Community" is the ASEAN motto.
4. 12th August is celebrated as ASEAN Day.
explanation: The ASEAN chair rotates alphabetically (1) and its motto is "One Vision, One Identity, One Community" (3). It is not decided by voting (2 wrong), and ASEAN Day is 8 August, not 12 August (4 wrong).

subject: Economy
stem: About the Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership (RCEP):
subs: 1. It is a comprehensive FTA between ASEAN members and ASEAN's FTA partners.
2. India opted out of RCEP.
explanation: RCEP is the mega-FTA among the ten ASEAN states and their FTA partners (1), and India withdrew/opted out in 2019 over concerns about trade deficits and import surges (2).

subject: Economy
stem: The Atmanirbhar Bharat scheme helps in:
subs: 1. Enhancing India's manufacturing capabilities and exports across industries.
2. Incentivizing foreign investments for domestic production.
explanation: Atmanirbhar Bharat ("self-reliant India") seeks to boost domestic manufacturing and exports (1) while also attracting investment for production in India (2) (e.g. via PLI schemes).

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the Districts as Export Hubs (DEH) initiative is not correct?
options: The aim of DEH is to boost exports of only selected districts.
India's Foreign Trade Policy 2023 reiterated the role of DEH.
DEH aims to promote exports by providing financial inclusion and logistical/infrastructural support.
explanation: DEH seeks to turn every district into an export hub, not just "selected" ones — so statement b is false.

subject: Economy
stem: Match (organisation – year of formation): A. European Union B. Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) C. ASEAN D. Free Trade Area of the Americas (FTAA) |

subject: Economy
stem: BREXIT refers to Great Britain leaving which body?
explanation: BREXIT = "Britain + exit", Britain's withdrawal from the European Union.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Global Environment Facility Grant Agreement (Aug 2017, World Bank, Ecosystems Service Improvement Project), which statement is not correct?
options: The project size is about USD 25 million.
It is financed entirely by the World Bank from its GEF Trust Fund.
explanation: The project duration was about 5 years, not 15 — so statement b is false. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Per the World Bank's Doing Business Report 2018, India's ranking improved (vs 2017) in which areas?
subs: 1. Paying taxes
2. Resolving insolvency
3. Starting a business
4. Getting electricity.
explanation: In the World Bank Doing Business Report 2018, India improved in Paying Taxes and Resolving Insolvency (statements 1 and 2), but did not improve in Starting a Business, so statement 3 is excluded - giving 1 and 2 only.

subject: Economy
stem: Which country is not a founding member of the New Development Bank?
explanation: The New Development Bank was founded by the BRICS nations (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa). Canada is not a member.

subject: Economy
stem: Arrange in descending order per the Global Human Development Index 2019:
subs: 1. Germany
2. USA
3. South Africa
4. India.
explanation: By HDI value: Germany > USA > South Africa > India (Germany and the USA are "very high," South Africa is "medium," India lower) — descending order 1, 2, 3, 4.

subject: Economy
stem: Which body assists a country through the Extended Fund Facility?
options: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
explanation: The Extended Fund Facility (EFF) is an IMF lending arrangement for countries with medium-term balance-of-payments problems needing structural reform.

subject: Economy
stem: Which report defines Sustainable Development as "development that meets the needs of the present without compromising future generations"?
options: Agenda-21 of the Rio Earth Summit
explanation: This famous definition is from the Brundtland Report, "Our Common Future" (1987).

subject: Economy
stem: About India's rank in the Global Innovation Index-2023 (WIPO):
subs: 1. India was placed at 40th rank.
2. India has been on a rising trajectory over 2015-2023.
explanation: India ranked 40th in GII 2023 (1) and has steadily climbed since 2015 (2) — both statements are correct.

subject: Economy
stem: In the July 2018 cabinet decision, the percentage increase in MSP was maximum for which crop?
explanation: In the 2018 MSP revision (which moved to the "cost + 50%" formula), Jowar (hybrid) recorded the largest percentage increase among the listed crops. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which factor is not considered in determining the Minimum Support Price?
options: Price trends in international and domestic markets
explanation: The CACP considers cost of production, demand-supply, market price trends, inter-crop parity, terms of trade, etc. — but not the cost-of-living index.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Public Distribution System:
subs: 1. Operated under the joint responsibility of Centre and States/UTs.
2. The FCI handles procurement, storage and transport of foodgrains to State Governments.
3. Both Centre and State jointly identify eligible beneficiaries.
4. Some States distribute additional items (pulses, edible oil) through PDS outlets.
explanation: The PDS is a joint Centre-State responsibility (1), with the FCI procuring/storing/transporting grain (2), and States adding extra commodities (4). Identifying beneficiaries is the State's responsibility, not joint — so statement 3 is wrong.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not an objective of the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY)?
options: To achieve convergence of investment in irrigation at the field level
To expand cultivable area under irrigation
To protect farmers against crop failure due to natural calamities
explanation: PMKSY is about irrigation ("Har Khet Ko Pani") and water-use efficiency ("More Crop Per Drop"). Protecting against crop failure is the job of crop insurance (PMFBY), not PMKSY.

subject: Economy
stem: The Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY), launched in 2015-16, is aimed at
options: making agriculture modern and globally competitive
improved access to irrigation and water efficiency
reducing agriculture's dependence on the monsoon
explanation: PKVY promotes organic farming through cluster-based, certified organic cultivation.

subject: Economy
stem: 1. The Government has discontinued the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana.
subs: 2. The Government has approved continuation of the Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme.
explanation: PMFBY continues (it has not been discontinued, so 1 is wrong); the Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme has been continued (2 correct).

subject: Economy
stem: The global initiative known as the "30 x 30 target" best reflects which statement?
options: At least 30% of the planet should be protected by 2030.
At least 30% of cultivated area under organic farming by 2030.
At least 30% of energy from renewables by 2030.
At least 30% of the poor lifted out of poverty by 2030.
explanation: The "30x30" biodiversity target aims to protect at least 30% of land and seas by 2030. (An environment item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about Anil Menon is correct?
options: An engineer who revolutionised organic farming
A diplomat in a UN agency
A physician selected by NASA to be an astronaut
explanation: Anil Menon is an Indian-American physician selected by NASA as an astronaut candidate. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not an objective of MGNREGA?
options: Providing up to 100 days of skilled labour in a financial year
explanation: MGNREGA guarantees up to 100 days of unskilled manual wage employment — not "skilled" labour — so statement a is the incorrect objective.

subject: Economy
stem: Social Mobilisation and Institution Development (SMID) is a major component of
options: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation
explanation: SMID — building Self-Help Groups and their federations — is a core component of the DAY-NRLM (Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana – National Rural Livelihoods Mission). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the sugar industry of Peninsular India is not correct?
options: High yield per hectare of sugarcane
Most mills in the Peninsula are located mainly along the east coast
explanation: Peninsular (especially Maharashtra) sugar mills are not concentrated only on the east coast — so statement d is false. The Peninsula does enjoy higher yield, sucrose content and a longer crushing season (cooler climate). (A geography item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about Antrix Corporation Limited is correct?
options: Under the administrative control of the Department of Space
Under the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Commercial arm of the Department of Science and Technology
explanation: Antrix is ISRO's commercial arm under the Department of Space.

subject: Economy
stem: Growth in production of which core industry was negative during 2015-16?
explanation: Among the eight core industries, natural gas output recorded negative growth in 2015-16. (A data item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The eBiz project (a Mission Mode Project under the National e-Governance Plan, guided by the DIPP) is implemented by
explanation: The eBiz single-window business-services portal was implemented by Infosys in PPP mode. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: As per the use-based classification of the Index of Industrial Production (IIP), the maximum weight is assigned to
explanation: In the IIP use-based classification, primary goods carry the largest weight (~34%).

subject: Economy
stem: India achieved 10% ethanol blending ahead of target. Which greatly benefits from this?
explanation: Ethanol in India is largely produced from sugarcane molasses/juice, so the ethanol-blending push directly benefits the sugar industry (an extra revenue stream). (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: About the cotton textile industry in India:
subs: 1. It has spread, over time, beyond Gujarat and Maharashtra.
2. Raw cotton is not a weight-losing raw material.
explanation: The cotton textile industry has spread across many states (1), and raw cotton is a "pure"/non-weight-losing raw material (the product retains its weight), allowing mills to locate near markets — so both statements are correct.

subject: Economy
stem: About MSMEs:
subs: 1. They form a dynamic sector providing large employment.
2. They need low capital cost and help industrialize backward regions.
3. They contribute enormously to socio-economic development. How many are correct?
explanation: All three statements correctly describe the role of MSMEs — major employment generators, low capital intensity, and broad socio-economic contribution.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Indian Institute of Entrepreneurship (IIE):
subs: 1. It was established in 1999 in Guwahati.
2. Its main aim is training, research and consulting for small and micro enterprises.
explanation: The IIE, Guwahati was established in 1993, not 1999, so statement 1 is wrong; its mandate is training, research and consultancy for small/micro enterprises (2 correct).

subject: Economy
stem: Correct statements about the Saakshar Bharat scheme:
subs: 1. A centrally sponsored scheme launched in the Eleventh Plan.
2. Applies to women in particular and disadvantaged groups in general.
3. Applies to persons above the age of 10 years.
4. Anchored with Panchayati Raj Institutions/local self-government.
explanation: Saakshar Bharat (adult literacy) was launched in the 11th Plan (1), targeted women/disadvantaged groups (2) and worked through PRIs (4). It covered persons aged 15+, not 10+, so statement 3 is wrong. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The Setu Bharatam Programme is for
options: building bridges across major rivers for goods transport
building bridges for safe and seamless travel on national highways
building roads to connect coastal areas
explanation: Setu Bharatam aims to make national highways free of railway level crossings by building rail over/under-bridges, and to repair/widen old bridges. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The projects under the Coastal Berth Scheme of the Sagarmala Programme are distributed over how many States?
explanation: Per PIB, Coastal Berth Scheme projects under Sagarmala were spread over eight states - Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Gujarat and West Bengal - not ten.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Bureau of Pharma PSUs of India (BPPI):
subs: 1. It is the implementing agency of the Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP).
2. It is registered as an independent society under the Societies Registration Act, 1860.
explanation: BPPI implements the Janaushadhi (generic-medicine) scheme (1) and is registered as a society under the 1860 Act (2) — both correct. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Identify the scheme: launched for old-age income security, protecting those aged 60+ against falling interest income, providing an assured pension via LIC.
explanation: PMVVY is an LIC-operated pension scheme giving senior citizens an assured return, shielding them from market interest-rate falls. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is the earliest launched scheme of the Government of India?
explanation: PMGSY was launched in 2000, well before the others (which came in 2014-15). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not a feature of the Ayushman Bharat scheme?
options: There is no cap on family size and age.
It includes pre- and post-hospitalization expenses.
A defined transport allowance per hospitalization is paid.
It provides a benefit cover of ₹10 lakh per family.
explanation: Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY) provides cover of ₹5 lakh per family per year, not ₹10 lakh — so statement d is false.

subject: Economy
stem: Correct statements about the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana:
subs: 1. Part of the Government's poverty-reduction strategy.
2. A centrally sponsored scheme for rural development.
3. Provides connectivity in rural areas.
explanation: PMGSY provides all-weather rural road connectivity (3) as a centrally sponsored rural-development scheme (2) that is part of the poverty-reduction strategy (1) — all three are correct. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is/are sub-mission(s) of the National Skill Development Mission (NSDM)?
subs: 1. Institutional training
2. Overseas employment
3. Leveraging of public infrastructure.
explanation: All three are sub-missions of the National Skill Development Mission - Institutional Training, Overseas Employment, and Leveraging Public Infrastructure - so the correct answer is 1, 2 and 3.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Second Five-Year Plan:
subs: 1. It was drafted under the leadership of K. N. Raj.
2. It proposed developing industries like electricity, railways, steel, machinery and communication in the public sector.
3. The drafters found balancing industry and agriculture very difficult.
4. The drafters found it really easy.
explanation: The Second Plan (the Mahalanobis plan) emphasised heavy industry in the public sector (2) and the drafters indeed found balancing industry and agriculture difficult (3). It was drafted under P. C. Mahalanobis, not K. N. Raj (1 wrong), and it was not "easy" (4 wrong).

subject: Economy
stem: Which set of States is at the bottom of the Composite SDG India Index 2020-21 (NITI Aayog)?
explanation: In the SDG India Index 2020-21, the lowest-scoring States were Bihar, Jharkhand and Assam. (A data-recall item worth confirming.)

subject: Economy
stem: The SDG India Index includes 17 SDGs for each State. Which is not included?
explanation: The SDGs include "Reduced Inequalities," not "Reduced Unemployment." Zero Hunger, Life Below Water and Responsible Consumption & Production are all SDGs.

subject: Economy
stem: Who was the Chairman of the Fourteenth Finance Commission?
explanation: The 14th Finance Commission was chaired by Y. V. Reddy (former RBI Governor); it recommended raising States' share of the divisible pool to 42%.

subject: Economy
stem: Which Indian cricketer was not inducted into the ICC Cricket Hall of Fame till July 2018?
explanation: Sachin Tendulkar was inducted only in 2019; by July 2018 Gavaskar, Kumble and Dravid (inducted 2018) were already in, but Tendulkar was not. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The Sasakawa Award of the United Nations is given for work in the field of
explanation: The UN Sasakawa Award recognises excellence in disaster risk reduction.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not a dimension of the Human Development Index?
options: Access to banking and other financial provisions
explanation: The HDI's three dimensions are a long and healthy life, knowledge (education), and a decent standard of living. Access to banking is not one of them.

subject: Economy
stem: Per UNDP's Human Development Report 2020, in which HDI components has India improved?
subs: 1. Life expectancy at birth
2. Expected years of schooling
3. GNI per capita
4. Mean years of schooling.
explanation: Over recent years India improved on all four HDI indicators — life expectancy, expected and mean years of schooling, and GNI per capita.

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the beneficiary target group of the Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan?
explanation: Launched during the COVID-19 lockdown, the Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan provided employment to migrant workers who had returned to their villages. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The computation of poverty in terms of Monthly Per Capita Consumption Expenditure (MPCE) based on the Mixed Reference Period was recommended by the
explanation: The Tendulkar Committee (2009) shifted poverty estimation to MPCE using a Mixed Reference Period and moved away from calorie-only norms.

subject: Economy
stem: Per the National MPI (NITI Aayog), a household is considered deprived if:
subs: 1. a single member is undernourished.
2. the BMI of a woman aged 15-49 is below 18.5 kg/m².
explanation: Under the nutrition indicator, a household is deprived if any member (including a low-BMI woman aged 15-49) is undernourished — so both statements are correct.

subject: Economy
stem: Regarding poverty in India:
subs: 1. The poverty line is fixed using a minimum level of food, clothing, footwear, fuel/light, education and medical requirements.
2. The accepted average calorie requirement is 2400 (rural) and 2100 (urban) calories per person per day.
explanation: Both are correct — the poverty line covers a basket of basic needs (1), and the calorie norms are 2400 (rural) and 2100 (urban) per capita per day (2).

subject: Economy
stem: About the Tendulkar Committee (2009) poverty estimates:
subs: 1. It used an all-India urban poverty-line basket as the reference to derive both rural and urban poverty.
2. It anchored the poverty line to the official food-calorie norms.
explanation: The Tendulkar Committee used the all-India urban poverty-line basket as reference (1) but deliberately moved away from calorie norms to a broader consumption basket — so statement 2 is wrong.

subject: Economy
stem: Which dimension is not included in the Human Development Index?
explanation: HDI uses life expectancy, mean and expected years of schooling, and GNI per capita — mortality rate is not a separate HDI indicator.

subject: Economy
stem: The Rangarajan Committee methodology for the poverty line incorporated:
subs: 1. A food component.
2. A normative level of expenditure for essential non-food items.
3. A behaviourally determined expenditure for other non-food items.
explanation: The Rangarajan Committee (2014) built the poverty line from a food component, a normative allowance for essential non-food items (education, health, clothing, etc.) and a behaviourally determined amount for other non-food spending — all three.

subject: Economy
stem: The mismatch in the regional or occupational pattern of job vacancies and the pattern of worker availability results in
explanation: Structural unemployment arises when workers' skills/location do not match available jobs (a mismatch in the structure of the labour market).

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005 is correct?
options: It ensures 175 days of employment to needy rural persons.
It gives higher wages to women workers.
People are generally given both skilled and unskilled jobs.
It is implemented only in rural areas of India.
explanation: MGNREGA guarantees up to 100 days of unskilled manual work in rural areas (with equal wages for men and women) — so only statement d is correct.

subject: Economy
stem: Unemployment that occurs due to changes in technology or in the demand for particular products is called
explanation: Structural unemployment results from longer-term shifts (technological change, changing demand) that make certain skills/industries redundant.

subject: Economy
stem: About the PLFS report 2023-24:
subs: 1. Agriculture's employment share rose from about 44% (2017-18) to about 46% (2023-24).
2. The share of female workers in agriculture increased over 2017-18 to 2023-24.
explanation: Both statements are correct — the agriculture share of employment rose and the share of female workers in agriculture increased. (Options referencing 3/4 are spurious.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which surveys are conducted by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation?
subs: 1. Survey on Social Consumption: Health
2. Comprehensive Modular Survey: Telecom and ICT skills
3. Periodic Labour Force Survey
4. Annual Survey on Unincorporated Enterprises.
explanation: All four surveys are conducted by MoSPI/NSO.

subject: Economy
stem: Match author with work: A. Sumit Sarkar B. Shahid Amin C. Ranajit Guha D. Bipan Chandra |
subs: 1. The Rise and Growth of Economic Nationalism in India
2. A Rule of Property for Bengal
3. The Swadeshi Movement in Bengal, 1903-1908
4. Event, Metaphor, Memory — Chauri Chaura, 1922-1992.
explanation: Sumit Sarkar → The Swadeshi Movement in Bengal (3); Shahid Amin → Event, Metaphor, Memory: Chauri Chaura (4); Ranajit Guha → A Rule of Property for Bengal (2); Bipan Chandra → The Rise and Growth of Economic Nationalism in India (1). (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee?
options: The member who has been the longest on the Committee
explanation: Under the rotational system (before the Chief of Defence Staff was created), the senior-most service chief — the one longest on the Committee — chaired the COSC. (A defence item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Correct statements about the Shiromani Gurudwara Prabandhak Committee (SGPC):
subs: 1. It began as the political wing of the Singh Sabhas in the late 19th century.
2. It was formed in 1920 as part of the Akali movement.
3. It was founded to reclaim control of Sikh shrines from government-manipulated loyalist committees.
4. It formed the Akali Dal to coordinate Jathas to reclaim the shrines.
explanation: The SGPC was formed in 1920 (2) to reclaim gurdwaras from loyalist committees (3) and set up the Akali Dal to coordinate Jathas (4). It was not a "political wing of the Singh Sabhas," so statement 1 is wrong. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not a component of the Revenue Receipts of the Union Government?
explanation: Disinvestment receipts are capital receipts (they reduce government assets), not revenue receipts. Taxes, dividends/profits and interest are revenue receipts.

subject: Economy
stem: The 7.6% growth rate of the Indian economy in 2015-16 was based on
options: Gross National Product at market prices
Gross Value Added at constant prices
Gross Domestic Product at market prices
Gross Domestic Product at constant prices
explanation: Real growth rates are measured using GDP at constant prices (which strips out inflation).

subject: Economy
stem: Amartya Sen was awarded the Nobel Prize for his contribution to
explanation: Amartya Sen won the 1998 Economics Nobel for his contributions to welfare economics (social choice, poverty and famine analysis).

subject: Economy
stem: The Ryotwari experiment in land revenue was started by
explanation: Captain Alexander Read (with Thomas Munro) introduced the Ryotwari system in the Baramahal region. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who chaired the committee to investigate the 1 November 2017 accident at the Feroze Gandhi Thermal Power Plant, Unchahar (UP)?
explanation: The Ministry of Power committee on the Unchahar (NTPC) accident was chaired by Shri P. D. Siwal. Dr. L. D. Papney was Member and Convenor, not the chairman - so the chairman is P. D. Siwal (option d).

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the Chairman of the Defence Planning Committee set up in April 2018?
options: The Chief of the Army Staff
explanation: The Defence Planning Committee (2018) is chaired by the National Security Advisor. (A defence item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The sub-prime crisis is associated with which event?
explanation: The US sub-prime mortgage crisis (2007-08) triggered the global financial crisis and the Great Recession.

subject: Economy
stem: Which canon of taxation was not advocated by Adam Smith?
explanation: Adam Smith's four canons were equality, certainty, convenience and economy. The canon of fiscal adequacy was added by later economists, not Smith.

subject: Economy
stem: What was the Dutt-Bradley thesis?
options: The INC Working Committee's role in independence
The Socialist party's foremost role in the anti-imperialist struggle
A Communist party document holding that the National Congress could play a great and foremost part in an anti-imperialist people's front
explanation: The Dutt-Bradley thesis (1936) was a Communist document arguing the Congress could be the chief vehicle of a broad anti-imperialist front. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who gave the concept of Human Development?
explanation: The Pakistani economist Mahbub-ul-Haq pioneered the Human Development concept and the HDI (with input from Amartya Sen).

subject: Economy
stem: The Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises consists of
options: the Department of Heavy Industry and the DIPP
the Department of Public Enterprises and the DIPP
the DSIR and the Department of Heavy Industry
the Department of Heavy Industry and the Department of Public Enterprises
explanation: The Ministry comprised the Department of Heavy Industry and the Department of Public Enterprises. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the Chairman of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM)?
explanation: Bibek Debroy chaired the EAC-PM at the time. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Formulation of policy on Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs) is the responsibility of
options: the Ministry of Law and Justice
the Department of Science and Technology
the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
the Ministry of Human Resource Development
explanation: IPR policy is handled by the DPIIT (Ministry of Commerce and Industry). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who gave evidence before the Joint Select Committee on the Government of India Bill, 1919 in favour of female franchise?
subs: 1. Mrs. Annie Besant
2. Mrs. Sarojini Naidu
3. Mrs. Hirabai Tata.
explanation: Annie Besant, Sarojini Naidu and Hirabai Tata all testified for female franchise before the committee. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the appointment of Members of Parliamentary Committees is correct?
options: The Members are appointed or elected on a motion made and adopted, or nominated by the Speaker (Lok Sabha) or Chairman (Rajya Sabha).
explanation: Committee members are either elected by the House (proportional representation) or nominated by the Speaker/Chairman — option d captures this. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The percentage by which the money the borrower pays back exceeds the money borrowed is called
explanation: The nominal interest rate is the actual percentage the borrower pays over and above the principal (not adjusted for inflation).

subject: Economy
stem: Which motion cannot be made while introducing an ordinary Bill in Parliament?
options: That the Bill be taken into consideration
That the Bill be circulated for eliciting public opinion
That the Bill be referred to a Select Committee
That the Bill be referred to a Joint Committee of the House without the concurrence of the other House
explanation: A Bill cannot be referred to a Joint Committee without the concurrence of the other House — that requires the other House's agreement. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statements are correct?
subs: 1. The Speaker may permit a Member to address the House in their mother tongue if they cannot adequately express in Hindi or English.
2. Business of Parliamentary Committees is transacted in Hindi or English.
3. The minutes of Parliamentary Committees are prepared invariably in Hindi or English.
explanation: All three are correct conventions of parliamentary procedure regarding language. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Under which name did the Russian Federation participate in the Tokyo Olympics 2020?
explanation: Due to doping sanctions, Russia competed as the "ROC" (Russian Olympic Committee) rather than under its national name/flag. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: 1. The Committee on Estimates is an ad hoc committee of Parliament.
subs: 2. The Committee on Railway Convention is a standing committee of Parliament.
explanation: The Estimates Committee is a standing (financial) committee (not ad hoc), and the Railway Convention Committee is an ad hoc committee (not standing) — so both statements are wrong. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: What is a "Unicorn Company" often mentioned in Indian news?
options: Any privately held startup valued over $1 billion
A PSU to be merged with another PSU
Privatization of a loss-making State-owned company
A foreign MNC operating in India with an Indian partner
explanation: A "unicorn" is a privately held startup valued at over US$1 billion.

subject: Economy
stem: In India, which body is responsible for implementing the Government's policy on all matters concerning telecommunications?
explanation: The Digital Communications Commission (formerly the Telecom Commission) takes policy decisions and implements them in the telecom sector. (TRAI is the regulator.) (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) is not correct?
options: It examines the Finance Accounts of the Government of India.
Fifteen members are elected by the Lok Sabha from amongst its members.
The Chairperson of the Committee is elected by its members.
The Speaker decides on continued membership in case of dual-committee membership.
explanation: The PAC Chairperson is appointed by the Speaker (from among Lok Sabha members) — not elected by the committee's members — so statement c is false. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the Solicitor General of India (SGI) is not correct?
options: The SGI is the Government's chief legal advisor.
The SGI is the secondary law officer of the country.
The post of SGI is a constitutional post.
The SGI is appointed by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet.
explanation: The SGI is a statutory/executive post, not a constitutional one (only the Attorney General is mentioned in the Constitution, Article 76) — so statement c is false. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which committee does not have members from the Rajya Sabha?
options: The Committee on the Welfare of SCs and STs
explanation: The Estimates Committee consists exclusively of Lok Sabha members (30 members), with no Rajya Sabha representation. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which was not part of the action programme of the Non-Cooperation Movement?
options: The Congress organisation was to reach down to the village/mohalla level.
Boycott of government-affiliated schools and colleges.
Taking control of the State's law-and-order machinery by the Congress Working Committee.
Surrender of titles and honours given by the government.
explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was about withdrawing cooperation (boycotts, surrender of titles, building Congress organisation) — not seizing the State's law-and-order machinery — so statement c is the odd one out. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which are included in the M1 definition of money?
subs: 1. Reserves
2. Currency
3. Time deposits
4. Demand deposits.
explanation: M1 = currency with the public + demand deposits (+ other deposits with RBI). Reserves and time deposits are not part of M1 (time deposits fall under broader money, M3).

subject: Economy
stem: About the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO):
subs: 1. One of its goals is to promote a new democratic, fair and rational international political and economic order.
2. Iran is a permanent member of the SCO.
explanation: Promoting a new, fairer international order is among the SCO's goals (1), and Iran became a full (permanent) member in 2023 (2) — both correct. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: 1. A rupee Vostro account is one that an Indian bank holds for a foreign bank in rupees, enabling domestic banks to provide international banking services.
subs: 2. Insurance density (average premium per capita) does not fully capture the financing gap on premature death because most Indian insurance products are savings-linked with a small protection component.
explanation: Both statements are correct — the description of a rupee Vostro account (1) and the limitation of insurance density as a protection-gap measure (2) are accurate.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC):
subs: 1. The Central Vigilance Commissioner is appointed by the President of India.
2. The committee recommending the appointment includes the Minister of Home Affairs.
3. In certain circumstances the Leader of the single largest opposition group in the Lok Sabha can be on the committee. How many are correct?
explanation: All three are correct — the CVC is appointed by the President on the recommendation of a committee comprising the Prime Minister, the Home Minister and the Leader of the Opposition (or the leader of the single largest opposition group) in the Lok Sabha. (A polity item.)
